text.text: *6 unresolved issues* with events in the last 24h  |  :red_circle: Critical: 1  |  :large_yellow_circle: Moderate: 2  |  :large_blue_circle: Trivial: 1  |  :white_circle: Noise: 2

• *<https://yung-tang-chou.sentry.io/issues/MIT-MAP-1B|MIT-MAP-1B>* — `PGRST205: Could not find table 'public.brand_slug_redirects' in schema cache`
  Events: 2  |  `GET /[locale]/brands/[slug]`  |  :new: New
  *Action:* Missing table is breaking every brand slug page request. Determine whether `brand_slug_redirects` was dropped, renamed (hint suggests `brand_reports`), or never migrated — then either run the missing migration or remove the reference.

• *<https://yung-tang-chou.sentry.io/issues/MIT-MAP-21|MIT-MAP-21>* — `NS_ERROR_FAILURE: No error message`
  Events: 6  |  `/:locale/brands/:slug`  |  :new: New
  *Action:* Firefox-specific failure on brand detail pages; 6 events clustered within ~1 hour. Reproduce in Firefox, inspect network requests for failed resource loads or fetch aborts.

• *<https://yung-tang-chou.sentry.io/issues/MIT-MAP-1C|MIT-MAP-1C>* — `Error: {code, details: null, hint, message}` (PostgREST, unserialized)
  Events: 2  |  `/:locale/brands/:slug`  |  :new: New
  *Action:* Unserialized PostgREST error on brand detail page — likely cascades from the MIT-MAP-1B schema issue. Examine full error body; resolving 1B may clear this.

• *<https://yung-tang-chou.sentry.io/issues/MIT-MAP-1J|MIT-MAP-1J>* — `PGRST103: Offset 96 requested, but only 90 rows exist`
  Events: 2  |  `GET /[locale]/brands`  |  :new: New
  *Action:* Pagination bounds bug — the brands list requests an offset beyond the available row count. Add a guard that caps the offset to the total row count before the API call.

• *<https://yung-tang-chou.sentry.io/issues/MIT-MAP-22|MIT-MAP-22>* — `SyntaxError: Unexpected end of JSON input`  |  Events: 1  |  `/_error`  |  :new: New  — Monitor only; single transient event on error page
• *<https://yung-tang-chou.sentry.io/issues/MIT-MAP-20|MIT-MAP-20>* — `SyntaxError: Unexpected end of JSON input`  |  Events: 1  |  `/_error`  |  :new: New  — Monitor only; single transient event on error page
